  "weights": {

    "R1.2",

    "R1.4",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which focus should this review prioritize? A) Technical/argument review (logic, evidence alignment, novelty: R1.2–R1.4), B) Writing/style and organization (R1.5–R1.6), C) Citation and scholarship audit (R1.7), or D) All of the above with equal weight.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The section advances theoretical and comparative claims whose premise-to-conclusion coherence should be checked for internal logical consistency.",
    "R1.3": "The experimental results are used to justify claims about faster convergence and theory-to-behavior translation, so claims must align with the reported evidence.",
    "R1.4": "The text asserts improvements over prior rates and a first-of-its-kind guarantee, requiring an assessment of novelty versus the nearest prior work.",
    "R1.7": "The comparison to Polyak (1967) and claims of state of the art hinge on accurate, sufficient citation and attribution.",
    "RX.1": "The review should state uncertainties where conclusions depend on unstated assumptions or incomplete methodological detail.",
    "RX.2": "The review must guard against endorsing overbroad claims such as general state-of-the-art superiority without adequate support.",
    "RX.3": "The review should remain scoped to evaluating this section rather than performing new analyses or edits beyond the request.",
    "RX.4": "Feedback should avoid recommending practices that could cause ethical issues (e.g., problematic human-rating procedures) or misuse.",
    "RX.5": "Each critique should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided text for clear provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus should this review prioritize? A) Technical/argument review (logic, evidence alignment, novelty: R1.2–R1.4), B) Writing/style and organization (R1.5–R1.6), C) Citation and scholarship audit (R1.7), or D) All of the above with equal weight.

rationale.R1.2: The section advances theoretical and comparative claims whose premise-to-conclusion coherence should be checked for internal logical consistency.
rationale.R1.3: The experimental results are used to justify claims about faster convergence and theory-to-behavior translation, so claims must align with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The text asserts improvements over prior rates and a first-of-its-kind guarantee, requiring an assessment of novelty versus the nearest prior work.
rationale.R1.7: The comparison to Polyak (1967) and claims of state of the art hinge on accurate, sufficient citation and attribution.
rationale.RX.1: The review should state uncertainties where conclusions depend on unstated assumptions or incomplete methodological detail.
rationale.RX.2: The review must guard against endorsing overbroad claims such as general state-of-the-art superiority without adequate support.
rationale.RX.3: The review should remain scoped to evaluating this section rather than performing new analyses or edits beyond the request.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid recommending practices that could cause ethical issues (e.g., problematic human-rating procedures) or misuse.
rationale.RX.5: Each critique should be traceable to specific sentences or claims in the provided text for clear provenance.
